evals.prompt: i want to start a new project called recipe-book, just use the defaults for everything
evals.expected_output: A working Next.js project with shadcn/ui, Tailwind v4, Prisma (sqlite), dark mode, and a polished landing page, all using default settings
expectations: At least 5 component files exist in src/components/ui/
src/app/page.tsx is customized (not default Next.js boilerplate)
package.json contains postinstall script with prisma generate

evals.prompt: Create a new app called dashboard-pro. I want postgresql for the database, the zinc theme, and install these components: button, card, input, label, table, tabs, dialog, dropdown-menu, avatar, badge, separator, sheet, tooltip
evals.expected_output: A working Next.js project configured with PostgreSQL, zinc base color theme, and all 13 specified shadcn components installed, with dark mode and Prisma
expectations: At least 5 component files exist in src/components/ui/
src/app/page.tsx is customized (not default Next.js boilerplate)
package.json contains postinstall script with prisma generate
table and tabs component files exist in src/components/ui/

evals.prompt: set me up with a new nextjs app with shadcn and prisma, call it bug-tracker
evals.expected_output: Either asks clarifying questions or proceeds with sensible defaults. End result is a working Next.js project with shadcn, Prisma, dark mode, and a landing page
expectations: At least 5 component files exist in src/components/ui/
src/app/page.tsx is customized (not default Next.js boilerplate)
package.json contains postinstall script with prisma generate
